Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“maybe the difficult technology to use is Unmanned Aerial Vehicle or UAV, I got it last week and we use it to to collect data collect data from buildings from gound and other other other type of agriculture, yeah agriculture. I am majoring in civil engineering and I have to do something with agriculture building and we use data to collect data from these buildings to information model, from information model to test our program such as energy energy related but UAV is different for me to use because I am not very familiar with technology. so, everytime I use it to practice data it will cost so much time for me to operate.”


Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Describe a piece of technology that you find difficult to use
You should say:
What you use it for
When you got it
How often you need to use it
And explain why you find this piece of technology difficult to use”

The piece of technology that I find particularly challenging to use is the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV), which I acquired last week. Primarily, I use it for collecting data from various structures, including buildings and agricultural sites. This is directly relevant to my field of study, as I am majoring in civil engineering with a particular focus on integrating agricultural aspects into building projects. The UAV is crucial for gathering detailed information, which is then utilized to create information models. These models assist in testing various programs, especially those related to energy efficiency.

However, despite its importance, I find the UAV difficult to operate due to my limited familiarity with such advanced technology. Its operation is not intuitive for me, and as a result, every attempt to practice and collect necessary data is time-consuming. This challenge is compounded by the fact that my coursework requires frequent use of this technology, thus highlighting a significant gap in my technical skills that I need to address to efficiently incorporate UAV data into our information modeling and program testing processes.

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“When thinking about this question, the first plant that comes to my mind is [name], it's a great plant that can use for medical and can improve our boost our baby and it's from many ancient maybe in our lost east of China. I have never use it before but in medias and newspaper, I saw the ancient just like people is it look people and it has medical value in our Chinese medical, it's usually used to be for food or just medical usages.”

Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Describe an important plant in your country
You should say

What it is

Where you see it

What it looks like

And explain why it is important”

When considering an important plant in my country, the first one that springs to mind is Ginseng. Ginseng is a remarkable plant known for its medicinal properties and is believed to offer health benefits, including boosting immunity which can be particularly beneficial for infants. It has a long history in traditional Chinese medicine, revered for centuries, and is thought to originate from the regions in the far east of China. While I personally have never used it, I've seen it featured in various media and newspapers. Visually, ginseng has a unique appearance, often resembling the shape of a human body, which perhaps contributes to its symbolic significance in Chinese culture. It is typically used either as a food supplement or directly for medicinal purposes. Its importance in our culture cannot be overstated, as it represents a bridge between the ancient herbal traditions and modern medical practices, highlighting its enduring value in Chinese healthcare and wellness.

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“No, I don't like eating chocolate. I used to eat chocolate very often but now I dn't like and it can make me fat and I don't want to be too fat and I made chocolate apart far from me.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Do you like eating chocolate? Why/Why not?”

No, I do not enjoy eating chocolate anymore. Although I used to consume it quite frequently, I have since developed a dislike for it, primarily because it contributes to weight gain, which I am keen to avoid. Consequently, I have decided to distance myself from consuming chocolate to maintain my health and fitness.

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“I prefer to digital books because they are portable and convenient if I go to go to the university to study if I have to I have to take some paper books, it will make my bag very heavy. So, if the books can can can turn to the e-book, it will make me feel comfortable and convenient.”

"I prefer digital books because they are portable and convenient, especially when I go to the university. Carrying physical books would make my bag very heavy. Therefore, having books in digital form, or e-books, makes my life more comfortable and convenient."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“This is because on the laptop, it is very easy for me to do some notes on my e-books and after that when I want to adjust my notes, I can the operation of my e-books make my modification easier”
"This is because using a laptop allows me to easily take notes on my e-books, and afterward, if I need to adjust my notes, the functionality of the e-books simplifies making these modifications."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Yes, of course. When I want to read more books in the future, if I use the physical book, it will take me a lot of place and money. If I use digital phones, I can save more moneys and I can frees more room to instead of put more other things.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Will you read more online in the future?”

"Yes, definitely. Opting for digital books in the future will save me both space and money compared to physical books, which require a lot of room and can be costly. By reading on digital devices, I can save money and free up space for other things."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“No, although the digital book have many advantage, but sometimes if we if we need a quick note, we the physical book is a better choice because once we book and write something on the paper and if and in the other hand, if we long time in screen, it will have negative effect on the eye and the paper book not likely taken the place of e-books.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Do you think paper books will disappear in the future?”

"No, although digital books offer many advantages, there are situations where physical books are the better choice, especially for taking quick notes. The act of physically writing something down on paper can be more immediate and tactile compared to digital notes. Additionally, spending extended periods looking at screens can have adverse effects on our eyes, whereas paper books do not pose such issues. Therefore, I believe that paper books are unlikely to be completely replaced by e-books in the future."

“Have you ever tried any exciting activities?”

Reflecting on my childhood, one exciting activity that stands out is attending a cycling camp. This happened when I was 5 years old, and it spanned the entire summer holiday. It was a completely new and thrilling experience for me at the time.

But if I have a chance to go to scuba diving, I will very willing to take part in it.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:

However, if I am presented with another opportunity to go scuba diving, I would be very eager to participate in it."

Thank you. Come up with one idiom for the above sentence.
"Better late than never, discovering English Partner has been a game-changer for my IELTS preparation."

This experience make me feel more interested in fish in the futures.”

The appearance and behavior of the fish captivated me, instilling a deep fascination with marine life that has continued to grow over time."
Come up with the spoken version of the above sentence.

"One of the best memories from when I was a kid is the time my parents took me to the aquarium. It was the first time I'd ever been to one, and seeing all those different kinds of fish and marine species up close was just, wow, amazing. I remember being so fascinated by how the fish looked and moved. That visit really sparked my interest in fish and anything related to marine life, and that's something that's stayed with me ever since."

"Yes, my parents frequently emphasized the importance of sharing my food with classmates. Initially, I didn't understand why I needed to share my things with others. But I eventually realized that my parents encouraged this behavior to help me build stronger relationships with my classmates."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:

Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What kind of things do you like to share with others?”

"When I was a child, I was more inclined to share snacks, like chocolates or sweets, with others. Now that I'm older, I tend to share things that are less significant, partly because there's a concern that some people might have intentions to take advantage or deceive me."
Thank you. Come up with 2 idioms for this answer and identify them, please.

1. "When I was young, I would happily *give the shirt off my back*, sharing sweets or chocolates with friends. This idiom highlights the extent of my generosity as a child, willing to share anything I had."

2. "Now, as an adult, I am more *guarded with my possessions*, sharing only things of lesser value. This shift is due to the *wolf in sheep's clothing* I sometimes encounter, where people might exploit generosity. This idiom paints a cautionary picture of being wary of those who may appear innocent but have ulterior motives."

“What kind of things are not suitable for sharing?”

"I believe that items of considerable value, such as those made from gold or silver which might have been purchased by parents, are not suitable for sharing. When you share something important or very expensive, there is a high risk that you may not get it back in the same condition, or at all."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“Of course yes. with the development of society and technology, compared to 20 years ago, people have dramatic change in living environment such as nowadays young people at young age they can go over the world by plane of high speed. And it's not a common thing 20 years ago. in addition, people can join a lot of activities like yoga, and skiing 20 years ago this activity was not popular in china, so nowadays young people have more activities they attend.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“Are there more activities for young people now than 20 years ago?”

"Absolutely, yes. With the advancements in society and technology over the past two decades, there has been a significant transformation in our living environment. For instance, today's young people have the ability to travel the world via airplanes or high-speed trains, an option that was not as readily available 20 years ago. Furthermore, activities like yoga and skiing, which were not very popular in China two decades ago, are now widely embraced, offering today's youth a broader range of activities to participate in."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“I think the most important thing parents do is set a good sample for their children such as if parents allowed their children don't play computer games and they should be strict with themselves. On the other hand, parents can use some reward to encourage children for example if their kids obey some rules and parents can buy some gift for their children.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“How can parents teach children to obey rules?”

"I believe the most crucial role parents play is setting a positive example for their children. For instance, if parents restrict their children from playing computer games, they themselves should adhere strictly to similar guidelines. On the flip side, parents can employ rewards as a form of encouragement. For example, if their children follow certain rules, parents can acknowledge this obedience by gifting them something special."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“In my opinion, youngst and older people like to visit natural places on the one hand older people don;t have a lot of work or study so they have more time to enjoy the natural environment for example in night the older people can dance and for for a walk in the park. on the other hand for some youngest they have more energetic and enthusiastic to experience the difference metro places they like to travel to different place.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What kind of people like to visit natural places?”

"In my opinion, both young and older individuals enjoy visiting natural places, but for different reasons. On one hand, older people, having less work or study commitments, have more time to appreciate the natural environment. For instance, they can enjoy dancing at night or taking leisurely walks in the park. On the other hand, younger individuals, with their abundant energy and enthusiasm, seek out natural places for the unique experiences they offer, often traveling to various destinations to satisfy their adventurous spirit."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“There are many differences between a city and a natural areas. For example, in cities there are many high building and beautiful beautiful decoration. However, in natural areas they have more area for there are more areas to farm. In addition, I think in natural area, there are fresh air because in the cities they have some air pollution.”
Also make sure this response is relevant to these questions and topic below:
“What are the differences between a natural place and a city?”

"There are several key differences between cities and natural areas. For instance, cities are characterized by their tall buildings and elaborate decorations. In contrast, natural areas offer expansive spaces more suited for farming and agriculture. Additionally, natural areas tend to have fresher air, as cities often struggle with air pollution due to higher levels of industrial activity and vehicle emissions."
